name: Urolithin A was safe and shifted mitochondrial biomarkers in older adults
summary: The first-in-human urolithin A study moves the track beyond animal evidence by showing safety, bioavailability, and mitochondrial biomarker movement in older humans.
statement: In healthy sedentary older adults, single and repeated urolithin A dosing was reported as safe and bioavailable, and 4 weeks at 500 mg or 1000 mg modulated plasma acylcarnitines and skeletal-muscle mitochondrial gene-expression signatures.
time_horizon: Single-dose cohorts and 4 weeks of repeated dosing
quantitative_note: The abstract identifies 500 mg and 1000 mg repeated-dose arms with acylcarnitine and skeletal-muscle mitochondrial gene-expression changes as secondary outcomes.
caveats: This was a safety and biomarker study, not a functional efficacy trial.
The trial was short and involved healthy sedentary older participants.
Commercial sponsor and author conflicts should be reviewed during source-fidelity assessment.
